The first edition features nurse-turned-exotic animal broker Tonia Haddix as she fights to retain custody of seven chimpanzees bred for entertainment.

programme.title: JFK: Destiny Betrayed
programme.desc: Oliver Stone's four-part documentary exploring the Kennedy assassination, and questioning the actions of the President's political enemies.

programme.title: Murder at the Cottage
programme.desc: Investigative documentary by Jim Sheridan. A town in West Cork is rocked when Sophie Toscan du Plantier is murdered and journalist Ian Bailey is arrested at the scene.

programme.title: Murder at the Cottage
programme.desc: Jim Sheridan talks to a key witness who called the Irish police back in 1996 with information about Sophie Toscan du Plantier's murder.

programme.title: Murder at the Cottage
programme.desc: False leads muddy the investigation into Sophie's murder. Despite having more than 50 initial suspects, no charges are made.

programme.title: The Commandant's Shadow
programme.desc: Documentary following the son of the man who was in charge of Auschwitz concentration camp as he confronts his father's involvement in the murder of more than a million Jews.

programme.title: Villeneuve Pironi: Racing's Untold Tragedy
programme.desc: The relationship between F1 drivers Gilles Villeneuve and Didier Pironi, team-mates whose friendship was torn apart after a controversial ending to the 1982 San Marino Grand Prix.

programme.title: Mysteries of Evolution
programme.desc: The importance of cooperation in the animal kingdom, where some creatures work together to be social while others put the welfare of the group above their own potential.

programme.title: Mysteries of Evolution
programme.desc: From the shark to the horseshoe crab, meet the planet's 'living fossils' - the creatures that have survived relatively unchanged for millions of years.

programme.title: Patrick Aryee's Wild World
programme.desc: Patrick takes a monumental adventure that explores the breathtaking wild world, beginning by asking whether people's appetite for salmon is wiping out the lynx.

programme.title: Patrick Aryee's Wild World
programme.desc: Patrick travels to Yellowstone National Park, where he discovers the positive, top-down domino effect that wolves have on their environment.

programme.title: Patrick Aryee's Wild World
programme.desc: Patrick is in Costa Rica to witness the annual mass nesting of sea turtles, as well as the predatory jaguars that come in for the kill.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: The Bay of Fundy on Canada's east coast, which is home to the world's highest tides - beneath which lies an intricate maze of invertebrate life.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Dr Alison Cronin and animal manager Jeremy Keeling return from Argentina with chimpanzees Sasha and Kangoo, and Tuan's orang-utans receive a treat.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A marmoset family heads to a new home, while Tya returns looking for love and the Barn Woollies welcome Baby Xenia to the group.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: The Indonesian archipelago in the heart of the Coral Triangle, an area recognised worldwide as the centre of marine biodiversity.

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Pack vs Pride
programme.desc: Julz goes on a lion-collaring mission and scout Simon goes fishing. The team witness a vigil for a badly injured Kakumbi pup.

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Pack vs Pride
programme.desc: The Kakumbi mourn the loss of two of their pups and the team witness a standoff between a leopard and a herd of angry elephants.

programme.title: Secrets of the Enchanted Waters
programme.desc: A look at Earth's most captivating bodies of water. Exploring the darkest depths of the sea to uncover the ocean's most mysterious inhabitants, and journeying across vast stretches of open water with humpback whales.

programme.title: Wildlife Icons
programme.desc: From dry deserts to the moist savannahs, springboks and impalas have established themselves as key species on the African continent.

programme.title: Big Beasts: Last of the Giants
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee investigates the world's largest animals, revealing why these creatures evolved into giants and how crucial they are to their habitats. He begins in the Americas, finding out more about the sperm whale, coming face to face with grizzly bears and getting up close and personal with an anaconda.

programme.title: Wild Philippines
programme.desc: Cameras follow turtles and tiger sharks roaming volcanic reefs, and tarsiers leaping through forests on the hunt.

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: Christmas Day in New Zealand means summer, and while the kiwi bird shuns the blisteringly hot sun, sea lions, penguins and dolphins make the most of the extra hours of daylight.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A sibling house-flipping team searches for an affordable property in Gulf Breeze, Florida that they can renovate, enjoy for a few months each year, and then rent to vacationing families seeking a beach getaway.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: After selling their farm in South Carolina, a couple retreats to Florida's beaches; they are ready to stop renting and buy a fixer-upper by the water, where they can create their dream home.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple who flips homes uses all their experience to buy and renovate an outdated beach house straight out of the 1970s. They put in plenty of sweat equity to keep the costs low and get the home ready for the next family of buyers.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A sibling house-flipping team searches for an affordable property in Gulf Breeze, Florida that they can renovate, enjoy for a few months each year, and then rent to vacationing families seeking a beach getaway.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A hardworking couple dreams of escaping Colorado's cold to find the perfect beachfront condominium; they use their real estate and remodeling skills to secure their own slice of paradise.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: With years of experience, Mark knows cabins that look bad on the outside can turn out to be perfect when salvaged for parts and pieces; he and the guys scout out some incredible log structures and turn them into extraordinary new homes.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark explores all the ways people repurpose the antique wood into flooring, wall coverings, decorative trim, furniture, cool crafts, and much, much more.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders are on the hunt for spare parts as they travel deep into one holler and high atop another to two different towns to save two different barns; along the way they find rare artifacts and visit a historic pioneer village.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders travel to save a fine example of a Pennsylvania bank barn; crafted from white oak, the timberframe features a style of diagonal bracing they've never encountered before.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders head to the Greenbrier River in West Virginia to build a pavilion; combining the elements of a timberframe structure and a classic log cabin.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders go to Lake Lure, North Carolina to build a log cabin garage right beside Russell's Fort, an American icon from 1775.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the Cabin Masters team attempt to restore a family camp that sisters Charlene Andersen and Pennilyn Dudley fondly recall many stories of from the last 50 years.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: For nearly 50 years, Mary and her five siblings created loving memories at their family cabin; Mary wants to create additional bedroom/sleeping space while expanding the bathroom to accommodate their large family.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: After purchasing land in 2006, a man set out to construct a cabin within 72 hours, the resulting camp is lacking bedrooms, so the Cabin Masters create a main bedroom, sleeping areas and separate bathrooms.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Two newlyweds call on the Cabin Masters to expand the 1957 Great Pond cabin they inherited to include enough space for their family, which includes three children, their spouses and six grandchildren.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family with a legacy of enjoying the lakes of Maine buys a 30-year-old gambrel garage with an efficiency apartment on the second floor.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Rooney family spends their summer with relatives and friends on Great Pond, but their camp is too small, and it's so dark that they can barely tell they're on a lake.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family purchases land on Sebec Lake and acquires an old shipping container they hope the team can transform into a dream cabin; the crew is challenged by the metal walls as they tackle the build offsite before transporting it to its forever home.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Wheeler family enlists the help of the team to renovate their old cabin so that it fits their growing kids; the crew removes a dead tree that threatens to fall on the property at any moment.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A lake-loving family outgrows their legacy cabin; the gang tackles the challenges of remodeling the mazelike layout as Ashley uses an heirloom for a special project, all in time for the family's annual 4th of July party.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family inherits a generational cabin they love on Sand Pond; along with the camp itself, they inherit a dark and damp building with a dated layout and lots of rot; the "Maine Cabin Masters" hammer, saw and sand their way through an overhaul.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family in Ryan's hometown has a pair of cabins, right on the water, that are in need of updates after 40 years of use; the Cabin Masters provide a facelift, including updated foundations.
